evals.prompt:
Run /jira-tickets --project audit-log-jt

JIRA MCP is NOT available. Instead of calling MCP, produce a dry-run plan showing exactly what you would create. Write the plan to docs/projects/audit-log-jt/jira-tickets-plan.md.

The plan must show:
- Epic: summary and description
- Each Story: summary, description (End Goal / Implementation Details / Out of Scope sections), work-type suffix, sizing estimate (// est: ~N days)

JIRA config is pre-set in docs/projects/audit-log-jt/.jira-config.md (project: PROJ, prefix: WE, label: Audit_Log).
Functional requirements are in docs/projects/audit-log-jt/functional-requirements-ea.md (status: approved).
evals.expected_output: jira-tickets-plan.md with one Epic and one Story per technical task (not one per user story). Each story has [API]/[UI]/[DB]/[OPS] prefix, sizing estimate, End Goal / Implementation Details / Out of Scope sections. EA blocker tasks flagged for manual dependency linking.

assertions.text: The plan shows significantly more stories than user stories (6 EA user stories should produce 10+ individual task stories, not 6)
Every story summary uses the [TEAM-SUFFIX] prefix format with a work-type suffix: [API], [UI], [DB], or [OPS]
Every story includes a sizing estimate comment: '// est: ~N days'
Every story description includes the three required sections: End Goal, Implementation Details, and Out of Scope
EA blocker stories (for OTQ-3, OTQ-4, OTQ-5 dependencies) are flagged in the plan — either as dependency link notes or explicit blocker callouts
The Epic summary follows the format 'EA: <Human-readable feature name>' (not the project slug 'audit-log-jt')

evals.prompt:
Run /jira-tickets --project eo-rule-editor-jt

JIRA MCP is NOT available. Instead of calling MCP, produce a dry-run plan showing exactly what you would create. Write the plan to docs/projects/eo-rule-editor-jt/jira-tickets-plan.md.

The plan must show:
- Epic: summary and description
- Each Story: summary, description (End Goal / Implementation Details / Out of Scope sections), work-type suffix, sizing estimate (// est: ~N days)

JIRA config is pre-set in docs/projects/eo-rule-editor-jt/.jira-config.md (project: PROJ, prefix: WE, label: Rule_Editor_History).
Functional requirements are in docs/projects/eo-rule-editor-jt/functional-requirements-ea.md (status: approved).
evals.expected_output: jira-tickets-plan.md with one Epic and one Story per technical task (3 user stories should produce 5+ individual task stories). Each story has [UI] or [OPS] prefix (no backend), sizing estimate, structured description. External dependency on EA Audit Log team noted.

assertions.text: The plan shows more stories than user stories (3 EA user stories should produce 4+ individual task stories)
Every story summary uses the [TEAM-SUFFIX] prefix format — primarily [UI] for this UI-only feature (no [API] or [DB] for new backend work)
Every story includes a sizing estimate comment: '// est: ~N days'
Every story description includes the three required sections: End Goal, Implementation Details, and Out of Scope
The EA Audit Log team external dependency is called out in at least one story's Implementation Details or as a blocker note
